heading: Division Directors for Diabetes, Endocrinology, and Metabolic Diseases, Digestive Diseases and Nutrition, and Kidney, Urologic, and Hematologic Diseases; functions
label: 285c–2 Division Directors for Diabetes, Endocrinology, and Metabolic Diseases, Digestive Diseases and Nutrition, and Kidney, Urologic, and Hematologic Diseases; functions
notes: Amendments2002—Subsec. (b)(1). substituted “Ruth L. Kirschstein National Research Service Awards” for “National Research Service Awards”.Pub. L. 107–2061993—Subsec. (b). substituted “the” for “the the” before “Division Director for Diabetes” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 103–43
amendments: Amendments2002—Subsec. (b)(1). substituted “Ruth L. Kirschstein National Research Service Awards” for “National Research Service Awards”.Pub. L. 107–2061993—Subsec. (b). substituted “the” for “the the” before “Division Director for Diabetes” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 103–43

children.label: A
children.text: developing a coordinated plan (including recommendations for expenditures) for each of the national research institutes within the National Institutes of Health with respect to research and training concerning diabetes, endocrine and metabolic diseases, digestive diseases and nutrition, and kidney, urologic, and hematologic diseases;

children.label: B
children.text: assessing the adequacy of management approaches for the activities within such institutes concerning such diseases and nutrition and developing improved approaches if needed;

children.label: C
children.text: monitoring and reviewing expenditures by such institutes concerning such diseases and nutrition; and

children.label: D
children.text: identifying research opportunities concerning such diseases and nutrition and recommending ways to utilize such opportunities.

The Director of the Institute shall transmit to the Director of NIH the plans, recommendations, and reviews of the Division Directors under subparagraphs (A) through (D) of paragraph (1) together with such comments and recommendations as the Director of the Institute determines appropriate.

carry out programs of support for research and training (other than training for which Ruth L. Kirschstein National Research Service Awards may be made under ) in the diagnosis, prevention, and treatment of diabetes mellitus and endocrine and metabolic diseases, digestive diseases and nutritional disorders, and kidney, urologic, and hematologic diseases, including support for training in medical schools, graduate clinical training, graduate training in epidemiology, epidemiology studies, clinical trials, and interdisciplinary research programs; andsection 288 of this title
establish programs of evaluation, planning, and dissemination of knowledge related to such research and training.
